ip_outcome_note: Depicted crescent moon-shaped Rock/Psychic creature with single dark eye matches Lunatone's canonical design exactly. National Pokedex no. 337. Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io API (swsh3-72) and Serebii Pokedex.

trivia_bullets.text: Darkness Ablaze (Sword & Shield series, released August 14, 2020) is the third S&S expansion, headlined by Charizard VMAX and Eternatus VMAX. Lunatone sits at card 072/189 in the printed set of 189 (201 total with secret rares). [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-72]

This is the second Lunatone in the inventory's DAA wave. The prior print (BUS-68, Shin Nagasawa art) was a Fighting-type with the Heal Block ability requiring Solrock in play — leaning into the sun/moon pair mechanic. This DAA print (Miki Tanaka art) drops the pairing requirement entirely: it is Psychic-type with standalone Future Sight and Psychic attacks, functioning independently of Solrock. Different artist, different typing, different design philosophy across two Sun & Moon-era prints three years apart. [PokemonTCG.io API: sm3-68 vs swsh3-72]
Flavor text: "The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It's active on the night of a full moon." [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-72]

Lunatone (National Pokedex no. 337) is classified as the Meteorite Pokemon. Lore holds it was first discovered at the site of a meteor crash and may have originated from outer space; its counterpart Solrock shares this origin. Both are Rock/Psychic-types found in Hoenn. [WebSearch snippet: Lunatone Bulbapedia]
sources: WebSearch snippet: Lunatone Bulbapedia via WebSearch

related_cards.name: Solrock
